_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years' experience
clinical_indications: Swelling, ecchymosis, and tenderness over the distal clavicle
Painful active and passive range of motion (ROM) of the shoulder
Neurologic examination observations of the shoulder and Upper limb documented
Radiographic evaluation includes true AP and axillary lateral views of the shoulder

clinical_thresholds.note: Specifically for Type II fractures with a higher rate of nonunion; Type I and III non-displaced fractures are managed non-surgically.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) confirming the diagnosis

claim.label: Post-procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) showing implant
Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge

ppd_preauth.q: Was the clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
Did the X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) confirming the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Post-procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) showing implant
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Is the Invoice/barcode of Implant used submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or X-ray report not indicative of a displaced clavicle fracture requiring surgery
Pre-procedure X-ray not labelled with patient ID, date, or side, or does not confirm the diagnosis
Post-procedure X-ray not submitted or not labelled with patient ID, date, or side, or not showing the implant
Invoice or barcode for the implant used is missing
Clinical notes missing details on physical examination, neurological assessment, or radiographic findings as per guidelines

clinical_key_pointers: Most distal clavicle fractures result from a fall onto the distal clavicle or a direct blow.
Stable fracture patterns generally heal uneventfully with nonsurgical management.
Unstable fracture patterns are often associated with longer time to union and notable nonunion rates.
Most commonly, these fractures occur within the middle third of the clavicle and exhibit some degree of displacement, though many midshaft clavicle fractures can be treated nonsurgically.
Distal clavicle fractures are classified into types I-V based on their location relative to the coracoclavicular (CC) ligaments and displacement characteristics.
surgical_options: Plate fixation (proven reliable and reproducible with high union rates and low complications)
additional_information.referral_criteria: Cases may require referral to the appropriate level of care for treatment under PMJAY and selection of corresponding Health Benefit Package.
contraindications: Nondisplaced Type I and Type III distal clavicle fractures (managed nonsurgically)
